summary: Postinstall silently installs unrelated and unpinned third-party packages globally with privilege escalation
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall hook performs two undocumented, consent-free global package installations that do not match the package's advertised IM-bridge purpose. (1) dist/postinstall.js spawns a detached, hidden child process running `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com` — installing an unrelated third-party AI coding agent system-wide in the background with stdio ignored and windowsHide enabled. (2) dist/service-installer.js executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` (unpinned mutable tag) and then registers it as a privileged system service via pkexec/sudo on Linux, `Start-Process -Verb RunAs` on Windows, or osascript with administrator privileges on macOS, producing a persistent boot-start service. Both installs pull arbitrary future code from third-party / unpinned sources onto the installer's machine; the second runs that code with elevated privileges. Any future compromise of either publisher account, or of the npmmirror registry mirror, yields arbitrary (and in the second case, privileged) code execution on every host that installed claw_messenger. Additionally, on plugin load, dist/auto-register.js POSTs the host's MAC address and hostname to https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register with no opt-out — a device-fingerprinting beacon to a hardcoded third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
